description: Authors state that making the dispersive coupling tunable would reduce gate infidelities by an additional order of magnitude and allow shorter gate times
value: Dispersive coupling tunability (xi/chi ratio) vs Logical gate infidelity
notes: Negative correlation: lower residual coupling (more tunable) leads to lower infidelity; authors cite reference [32] for tunable coupler implementation

description: Authors show that increasing readout speed (shorter τro, higher n̄r) leads to dramatically increased leakage rates while fidelity and repeatability remain approximately constant
value: readout duration / photon number vs readout-induced leakage rate per readout
notes: Strong negative correlation: leakage increases from 0.12% to 7.76% as τro decreases from 240 ns to 100 ns; fidelity and repeatability do not track this trend

description: Authors state that increasing measurement error reduces the ratio r (fractional Bell fidelity improvement) from distillation
value: Measurement error rate m vs Fractional Bell fidelity improvement r = Fa/Fb
notes: Negative correlation: higher measurement error reduces distillation benefit
term_b: Fractional Bell fidelity improvement r = Fa/Fb

description: Parity lifetime depends strongly on readout resonator frequency; higher frequency resonators require fewer photons for pair-breaking and thus cause QP generation at lower photon numbers
value: Readout resonator frequency ωr/2π ∈ {46, 34, 25, 20} GHz vs Charge-parity lifetime Tg and Te vs photon number n̄
notes: Higher resonator frequency causes onset of QP generation at lower photon numbers; the number of photons required for pair-breaking equals ⌈2Δ/ℏωr⌉
term_b: Charge-parity lifetime Tg and Te vs photon number n̄

description: Authors state that larger gap difference delta_Delta yields lower burst detection rate
value: Gap difference delta_Delta/h vs Burst detection rate (min^-1)
notes: Negative correlation: big-delta device (8.2 GHz) has 0.57 min^-1 vs small-delta device (0 GHz) at 2.76 min^-1, factor ~5 reduction

description: Authors state that smaller JJ asymmetry values cause the dispersive shift χlogical to drop linearly while χerasure remains relatively stable
value: kite JJ asymmetry δEJk/EJk vs χlogical and χerasure dispersive shifts
notes: χlogical scales linearly with asymmetry; χerasure is relatively insensitive to asymmetry; results in strong suppression of erasure-induced logical error perr ∝ χ²logical at small asymmetry

description: Authors state that increasing electrode-to-AlN-ring gap size reduces piezoelectric loss and improves microwave quality factor, suppressing thermal noise without substantially compromising transduction efficiency (g²eo·Qm figure of merit remains approximately flat with increasing cladding thickness)
value: SiO2 cladding thickness (electrode-AlN gap) vs Piezoelectric-limited microwave Qm and thermal noise occupancy n̄_mode
notes: Increasing gap increases Qm (positive) and reduces noise (positive) while geo decreases (negative), but net figure of merit g²eo·Qm is approximately constant — authors state noise can be reduced without compromising efficiency
term_b: Piezoelectric-limited microwave Qm and thermal noise occupancy n̄_mode

description: Authors state that the ratio IIP3/P1dB varies by an order of magnitude within the amplifier bandwidth, linked to nonuniform IIP3 across bandwidth
value: IIP3 as function of frequency vs P1dB compression power as function of frequency
notes: IIP3 variation is much smaller than P1dB variation across bandwidth, so their ratio varies by order of magnitude; positive correlation between frequency position in band and compression power
term_b: P1dB compression power as function of frequency

description: Authors state that CPMG T2,CPMG increases as N^0.6 for N > 100 pi-pulses, approaching 2T1 limit
value: Number of CPMG pi-pulses (N) vs T2,CPMG
notes: Positive correlation following power law N^0.6 for N > 100; saturates near 2T1

description: Authors state subharmonic Rabi rate scales as cube of drive amplitude, enabling rapid increase in gate speed with moderate power increase
value: Drive amplitude (βVd) vs Subharmonic Rabi rate (Ω)
notes: Cubic (power-law) positive correlation: 250x increase in Rabi rate for 7.1x increase in drive amplitude

description: Authors state ac-Stark shift scales as square of drive amplitude
